elements.sent-2411-001: In this paper, $f(R,\lm, T)$ gravity is considered.
elements.sent-2411-002: It is a generalization of the theories $f(R,T)$ and $f(R, \lm)$.
elements.sent-2411-003: This modified theory of gravity exhibits strong geometry-matter coupling.
elements.sent-2411-004: The problem of causality and its violation is verified in this model.
elements.sent-2411-005: Such analysis is carried out using G\"{o del-type solutions considering different types of matter.}
elements.sent-2411-006: It is shown that this model allows both causal and non-causal solutions.
elements.sent-2411-007: These solutions depend directly on the content of matter present in the universe.
elements.sent-2411-008: For the non-causal solution, a critical radius is calculated, beyond which causality is violated.
elements.sent-2411-009: Taking different matter contents, an infinite critical radius emerges that leads to a causal solution.
elements.sent-2411-010: To obtain a causal solution, a natural relationship arises between the parameters of the theory.
elements.sent-2411-011: The discovery of the accelerated expansion of the universe in the late 1990s \cite{Riess, expansao_ace2, Knop, Ama, Wein led to intense investigation in the search to explain this phenomenon, since a consistent explanation cannot be addressed by general relativity.}
elements.sent-2411-012: To explain this late cosmic acceleration, two proposals have been explored: (i) add new components of matter and energy to the theory of general relativity, namely, dark constituents of the universe; (ii) modify or generalize the Einstein-Hilbert action considering that gravitational action is an arbitrary function that may depend on the geometry and/or of the matter.
elements.sent-2411-013: In this work, the theory under investigation is $f(R, L_m, T)$ gravity \cite{harko .}
elements.sent-2411-014: The $f(R, L_m, T)$ gravity is a class of modified gravity theories developed to explain the late-time accelerated expansion of the universe, often referred to as the "dark coupling approach".
elements.sent-2411-015: Two additional approaches exist: (i) the dark components model, which extends Einstein's field equations by incorporating two extra terms into the total energy-momentum tensor of the universe, representing dark energy and dark matter separately; and (ii) the dark gravity framework, which offers a purely geometric interpretation of gravitational phenomena.
elements.sent-2411-016: The key idea behind this framework, i.e. dark coupling approach, is to replace the standard Hilbert-Einstein Lagrangian density - which treats the curvature and matter contributions separately and additively - with a more general algebraic formulation that couples these components more intricately.
elements.sent-2411-017: In this model, the Lagrangian is an arbitrary function of the Ricci scalar ($R$), the matter Lagrangian ($L_m$), and the trace of the energy-momentum tensor ($T$).
elements.sent-2411-018: This approach naturally introduces a non-minimal interaction between matter and spacetime geometry, allowing for various forms of coupling, such as between the Ricci scalar and the trace of the energy-momentum tensor.
elements.sent-2411-019: In this context, $f(R, L_m, T)$ gravity unifies two distinct modified gravity models: $f(R, T)$ and $f(R, L_m)$.
elements.sent-2411-020: Moreover, it introduces a non-minimal coupling of matter to geometry through both the matter Lagrangian and the trace of the energy-momentum tensor.
elements.sent-2411-021: A significant feature of this theory is that the resulting modifications apply non-trivially to all types of matter.
elements.sent-2411-022: As limiting cases this theory recovers the $f(R)$, $f(R, L_m)$ and $f(R, T)$ gravity theories.
elements.sent-2411-023: It is important to emphasize that all alternative theories proposed as gravitational theory must be intensively tested.
elements.sent-2411-024: A classical test consists of checking whether all exact solutions of general relativity are contained in this new model.
elements.sent-2411-025: Here causality and its violation are investigated.
elements.sent-2411-026: To address the challenge of causality within the domain of $f(R,\lm, T)$ gravity, a comprehensive investigation is performed using the G\"{o del-type solution \cite{tipo_godel1 .}}
elements.sent-2411-027: This metric is an extension of Kurt G\"{o del solution, proposed in 1949 \cite{godel .}}
elements.sent-2411-028: It represents an exact solution that incorporates rotating matter within the domain of general relativity.
elements.sent-2411-029: The main characteristic of this metric is its ability to accommodate Closed Timelike Curves (CTCs).
elements.sent-2411-030: CTCs are curves that lead to causality violation.
elements.sent-2411-031: A traveler on this curve can return to their past, at least theoretically.
elements.sent-2411-032: However, it is important to note that this is a global point of view since locally the general relativity satisfies the same conditions as the special theory of relativity, that is, the causality is preserved.
elements.sent-2411-033: On the other hand, CTCs and causality violation are not exclusive to G\"{o del-type solutions, this is a phenomenon whose presence extends beyond the limits of the G\"{o del solution.}}
elements.sent-2411-034: These CTCs manifest in a variety of cosmological models, such as the Kerr black hole \cite{ctc2 , Van-Stockum model \cite{Van , cosmic strings \cite{ctc1 , among others.}}}
elements.sent-2411-035: To further explore causality violation, the G\"{o del-type metric allows the calculation of a critical radius $r_c$, a parameter that demarcates regions of causal and non-causal behaviors.}
elements.sent-2411-036: As the study of causality is a classical test for alternative models of gravitation, the G\"{o del and type-G\"{o del solutions have been studied in several modified gravity theories.}}
elements.sent-2411-037: Such an investigation took place in $f(R)$ theory \cite{fr_and_godel , in $k$-essence theory \cite{kessence_and_godel , in Chern-Simons gravity \cite{chersimon_and_godel1, chersimon_and_godel2 , in $f(T)$ gravity \cite{ft_and_godel , in $f(R,T)$ gravity \cite{frt_and_godel , in bumblebee gravity \cite{bumblebeee_and_godel , in Horava-Lifshitz gravity \cite{horava_and_godel , in Brans-Dicke theory \cite{brans_and_godel , in $f(R,Q)$ gravity \cite{frq_and_godel , in $f(R,T)$ Palatini gravity \cite{frt_palatini , in $f(R,\phi,X)$ \cite{fr_phi_X , among others.}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-038: In this paper, the main objective is to investigate whether $f(R,\lm, T)$ gravity permits a G\"{o del-type solution, which is an exact solution in general relativity.}
elements.sent-2411-039: Specifically, we aim to explore if this new gravitational model can accommodate such a solution.
elements.sent-2411-040: Different types of matter content are considered, and the analysis is divided into four cases: (i) a perfect fluid; (ii) an electromagnetic field; (iii) a combination of a perfect fluid and an electromagnetic field; and (iv) a scalar field.
elements.sent-2411-041: Within this framework, both causal and non-causal regions are identified.
elements.sent-2411-042: In each case, a direct comparison with the corresponding general relativity solution is made to evaluate how $f(R,\lm, T)$ gravity modifies or preserves the G\"{o del-type behavior.}
elements.sent-2411-043: The present paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-044: In Section II, $f(R,\lm, T)$ gravity is introduced and the field equations are derived.
elements.sent-2411-045: In Section III, the G\"{o del-type metric is discussed.}
elements.sent-2411-046: In order to solve the field equations, different types of matter content are considered.
elements.sent-2411-047: Taking a perfect fluid as matter content the standard G\"{o del solution is obtained.}
elements.sent-2411-049: A critical radius is calculated and the influence of $f(R,\lm, T)$ gravity on this quantity is displayed.
elements.sent-2411-050: Considering an electromagnetic field, a combination of a perfect fluid plus an electromagnetic field and a scalar field as matter content, causal solutions are found.
elements.sent-2411-051: Furthermore, to obtain a causal solution a new restriction is required.
elements.sent-2411-052: In Section IV, we investigate a specific case of $f(R,\lm)$ gravity, referred to as exponential $f(R,\lm)$ gravity, considering three types of matter content.
elements.sent-2411-053: In Section V, some remarks and conclusions are presented.
elements.sent-2411-054: In this section, the $f(R,\lm, T)$ theory is presented and its field equations are obtained.
elements.sent-2411-055: The action that describes this gravitational theory is defined as \begin{equation}S= \frac{1}{16\pi} \int f(R,\lm, T) \sqrt{-g} \ d^4x + \int \lm \sqrt{-g} \ d^4x,\end{equation} where $f(R,\lm, T)$ is an arbitrary function of the Ricci scalar $R$, of the trace of the energy-momentum tensor $T$, with $T = g^{\mu\nu T_{\mu\nu}$, and of the Lagrangian density of ordinary matter, $\lm$, respectively.}
elements.sent-2411-056:

u}} = -2  rac{\partial \lm}{\partial g^{\mu

u} \lm.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-057: In order to obtain the field equations, the variation of the action with respect to the metric tensor is taken.
elements.sent-2411-058: Then \begin{equation}\delta S = \frac{1}{16\pi} \int d^4x \pr{\delta \sqrt{-g} f + \sqrt{-g} \pc{f_R \delta R + f_T\delta T + f_L \delta L_m }} + \int d^4x \delta \pc{\sqrt{-g} \lm}\label{eq3}\end{equation} with $f_R \equiv \frac{\partial f {\partial R}$, $f_T \equiv \frac{\partial f {\partial T}$ and $f_L \equiv \frac{\partial f {\partial \lm}$.}}}

elements.sent-2411-064: The main objective of this work is to investigate whether the causality violation described by G\"{o del-type universes persists in this gravitational theory as allowed in general relativity.}
elements.sent-2411-065: The study will be developed for different contents of matter.
elements.sent-2411-066: The G\"{o del-type solution represents a generalization of the G\"{o del metric.}}
elements.sent-2411-067: The G\"{o del universe is an exact solution to Einstein field equations for a homogeneous rotating universe.}
elements.sent-2411-068: This solution introduces the possibility of Closed Time-like Curves (CTCs), which allow for causality violations.
elements.sent-2411-069: Consequently, this space-time allows theoretical time travel.
elements.sent-2411-070: In order to obtain more details about causality violation, the G\"{o del-type metric is considered \cite{tipo_godel1 .}}
elements.sent-2411-071: The line element that describes this universe is given by \begin{equation}\label{type_godel} ds^2 = -dt^2 - 2H(r) dtd\phi + dr^2 + \left(D^2(r)-H^2(r)\right) d\phi^2 + dz^2,\end{equation} where $H(r)$ and $D(r)$ are functions that satisfies the relations \bea \frac{H'(r) {D(r) =2\omega \quad \mathrm{and}\quad \frac{D''(r) {D(r) =m^2.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-072: It is worth noting that the parameters $\omega$ and $m^2$ are free parameters that characterize all the properties of the metric.
elements.sent-2411-073:
Theses parameters define three distinct classes: hyperbolic ($m^2>0, \omega 
eq 0$), trigonometric ($m^2<0, \omega 
eq 0$), and linear ($m^2=0, \omega 
eq 0$) \cite{tipo_godel1 .}
elements.sent-2411-074: Here only the hyperbolic class is considered, since this class contains the standard G\"{o del universe.}
elements.sent-2411-075: For this class, the functions $H(r)$ and $D(r)$ are given as \begin{equation}H(r) = \frac{4\omega}{m^2} senh^2 \left(\frac{mr}{2} \right),\label{condition1} D(r) = \frac{1}{m} senh(mr)\label{condition2}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-076: The critical radius, which defines the existence of Closed Time-like Curves (CTCs), is an important quantity that can be derived from the G\"{o del-type solution.}
elements.sent-2411-077: It is defined as \begin{equation}r_c = \frac{2}{m} \sinh^{-1} \left(\frac{4\omega^2}{m^2}-1\right)^{-1}.\label{cr}\end{equation} This expression leads to two interesting relationships between the parameters $\omega^2$ and $m^2$.
elements.sent-2411-078: Firstly, when $m^2=2\omega^2$, the G\"{o del solution exhibits a finite critical radius given by \begin{equation}r_c = \frac{2}{m} \sinh^{-1} \left(1\right).\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-080: Secondly, if $m^2=4\omega^2$, the critical radius becomes infinite.
elements.sent-2411-081: In other words, this condition results in a causal solution.
elements.sent-2411-082:
For simplicity, a new basis, known as a local Lorentz co-frame, is chosen, transforming the metric into the form \cite{tipo_godel1 ,  egin{equation}ds^2 = \eta_{AB} 	heta^A 	heta^B =(	heta^{(0)})^2 - (	heta^{(1)})^2 -(	heta^{(2)})^2 - (	heta^{(3)})^2, \label{frame}\end{equation} where $\eta_{AB}$ represents the Minkowski metric, and $ 	heta^A = e^A\ _{\mu}\ dx^\mu$ with  egin{equation}	heta^{(0)} = dt + H(r)d\phi,
onumber 	heta^{(1)} = dr, 
onumber\ 	heta^{(2)} = D(r)d\phi,
onumber 	heta^{(3)} = dz. \label{coframe}\end{equation} Here, $e^A\ {\mu}$ corresponds to the tetrad that satisfies the relation $ e^A\,_\mu e^\mu\,_B=\delta^A_B$.}
elements.sent-2411-083: The capital Latin letters are used to label Lorentz indices ranging from 0 to 3.
elements.sent-2411-084: In the local Lorentz co-frame (\ref{coframe ), the non-zero Ricci tensor components are $R_{(0)(0) = 2\omega^2, \ R_{(1)(1) = \ R_{(2)(2) = 2\omega^2 - m^2.$ The Ricci scalar is $R = 2(m^2 - w^2)$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-085: Now, the objective is to use these quantities and solve the Einstein field equations (\ref{eq_campo ) choosing different matter contents.}
elements.sent-2411-086: In this subsection, the G\"{o del-type metric is analyzed in $f(R,\lm, T)$ gravity considering the perfect fluid as the matter content.}
elements.sent-2411-087: The energy-momentum tensor that describes the perfect fluid is given as \begin{eqnarray}T_{AB}=(\rho+p)u_A u_B-p\eta_{AB},\label{EMT}\end{eqnarray} with $\rho$ being the energy density, $p$ the pressure and $u_A = (1,0,0,0)$ the four-velocity of the fluid.
elements.sent-2411-088: The non-zero components of the energy-momentum tensor are $T_{00 = \rho , T_{11 = T_{22 = T_{33 = p $.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-089: The Lagrangian that describes the perfect fluid is defined as $L_M = -p$.
elements.sent-2411-090: The field equation Eq. (\ref{eq_campo ) in tangent space becomes \begin{equation}\label{eq_campo_tangente} f_R R_{AB} -\frac{1}{2} \pr{f-(f_L + 2f_T)\lm} \eta_{AB} + (\eta_{AB} \Box - \nabla_ A \nabla_B)f_R = \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2} (f_L + 2 f_T)} T_{AB} + f_T \tau_{AB}.\end{equation}.}
elements.formula-2411-017: \begin{equation}\label{eq_campo_tangente} f_R R_{AB} -\frac{1}{2} \pr{f-(f_L + 2f_T)\lm} \eta_{AB} + (\eta_{AB} \Box - \nabla_ A \nabla_B)f_R = \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2} (f_L + 2 f_T)} T_{AB} + f_T \tau_{AB}.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-091: Taking into account the perfect fluid, the tensor $\tau_{AB}$ becomes zero.
elements.sent-2411-092: In addition, the derivatives of the function $f_R$ are zero, since the Ricci scalar is constant.
elements.sent-2411-093: Therefore, the field equations are given as \begin{equation}2 f_R \omega^{2} - \frac{1}{2} f = \rho \kappa + (\rho + p)(\frac{1}{2} f_L + f_T ),\label{m} (2\omega^2 - m^2)f_R + \frac{1}{2} f = \kappa p \label{eq1_campo}, \frac{1}{2} f = \kappa p\label{eq2_campo}.\end{equation}. Eqs.
elements.formula-2411-018: \begin{equation}2 f_R \omega^{2} - \frac{1}{2} f = \rho \kappa + (\rho + p)(\frac{1}{2} f_L + f_T ),\label{m} (2\omega^2 - m^2)f_R + \frac{1}{2} f = \kappa p \label{eq1_campo}, \frac{1}{2} f = \kappa p\label{eq2_campo}.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-094: This condition defines the G\"{o del solution.}
elements.sent-2411-095: Then the causality violation is allowed in this gravitational theory.
elements.sent-2411-096: The remaining equation Eq. (\ref{m ) provides \begin{equation}m = \pr{\frac{(\rho + p)(\frac{1}{2}f_L + f_T) +\frac{1}{2}f + \kappa \rho }{f_R}}^{1/2}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-097: Then the critical radius in $f(R,T, \lm)$ gravity is \begin{eqnarray}r_c=2\sinh^{-1}(1)\pr{\frac{f_R}{(\rho + p)(\frac{1}{2}f_L + f_T) +\frac{1}{2}f + \kappa \rho } }^{1/2}.\end{eqnarray}.
elements.sent-2411-098: This result shows that the critical radius, beyond which the causality violation explicitly occurs, depends on the form of the function $f(R, \lm, T)$ and its derivatives, $f_R, f_L$ and $f_T$.
elements.sent-2411-099: This result is a generalization of the study developed for $f(R)$ and $f(R,T)$ gravity.
elements.sent-2411-100: In short, assuming the perfect fluid as the unique matter of the universe, the causality violation arises naturally.
elements.sent-2411-101: Now the question is: do other types of matter lead to the same consequence? Let us carry out this investigation in the next subsections.
elements.sent-2411-102: In an attempt to find a causal solution for the G\"{o del-type metric in $f(R, \lm, T)$ gravity the electromagnetic field is considered as the content of matter.}
elements.sent-2411-103: For simplicity, is assumed that the electromagnetic field is aligned on the $z$-axis and depends on $z$ \cite{tipo_godel1 .}
elements.sent-2411-104: For this choice, the non-vanishing components of the electromagnetic tensor in the flat space-time are \begin{equation}F_{(0)(3)}=-F_{(3)(0)}=E(z)\quad\mathrm{e}\quad F_{(1)(2)}=-F_{(2)(1)}=B(z),\label{27}\end{equation} where $E(z)$ and $B(z)$ are solutions of Maxwell equations given by \begin{equation}E(z) =E_0\cos\left[2\omega(z-z_0)\right],\label{28} B(z) =E_0\sin\left[2\omega(z-z_0)\right],\label{29}\end{equation} with $E_0$ being the amplitude of the electric and magnetic fields and $z_0$ is an arbitrary constant.
elements.sent-2411-105: Thus, the non-zero components of the energy-momentum tensor associated with the electromagnetic field are given by \begin{equation}\label{energy_sol} T_{(0)(0)}^{EM} = T_{(1)(1)}^{EM} = T_{(2)(2)}^{EM} = \frac{E_0^2}{2}, \quad\quad T_{(3)(3)}^{EM} = - \frac{E_0^2}{2}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-106: For this type of choice, the Lagrangian that describes the electromagnetic field is \bea L_M = -\frac{1 {4 F_{AB F^{AB = \frac{1 {2 (B^2 - E^2).}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-107: Then, the field equations (\ref{eq_campo_tangente ) become \begin{equation}4\omega^2f_R - f = -\frac{1}{2}(B^2-E^2) ( f_L + 2f_T) + E_0^{2} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2} (f_L +2f_T)} -2E^{2} f_T, 2(2\omega^2 - m^2)f_R + f = \frac{1}{2}(B^2-E^2) ( f_L +2f_T) + E_0^{2} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2} (f_L + 2f_T)} - 2B^2 f_T, \label{eq2eletro} - f = - \frac{1}{2}(B^2-E^2) (f_L + 2f_T) + E_0^{2} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2}(f_L + 2f_T) }- 2E^{2} f_T . \label{eq3eletro}\end{equation} Eqs.}
elements.formula-2411-024: \begin{equation}4\omega^2f_R - f = -\frac{1}{2}(B^2-E^2) ( f_L + 2f_T) + E_0^{2} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2} (f_L +2f_T)} -2E^{2} f_T, 2(2\omega^2 - m^2)f_R + f = \frac{1}{2}(B^2-E^2) ( f_L +2f_T) + E_0^{2} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2} (f_L + 2f_T)} - 2B^2 f_T, \label{eq2eletro} - f = - \frac{1}{2}(B^2-E^2) (f_L + 2f_T) + E_0^{2} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2}(f_L + 2f_T) }- 2E^{2} f_T . \label{eq3eletro}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-108: Considering that $f_R > 0$ and \begin{equation}f_T(E^2 + B^2) > E_0^{2} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2} (f_L +2f_T)},\end{equation} a causal G\"{o del-type solution, i.e., $m^2 = 4 \omega^2$ is allowed.}
elements.sent-2411-109: This implies that the critical radius, defined in Eq. (\ref{cr ), becomes infinity.}
elements.sent-2411-110: Therefore, it is possible a causal G\"{o del-type solution in $f(R, \lm, T)$ gravity when the matter content is an electromagnetic field.}
elements.sent-2411-111: Here let us investigate the consistency of the G\"{o del-type universe in $f(R, \lm, T)$ gravity considering as matter content a combination of perfect fluid and eletromagnetic field.}
elements.sent-2411-112: The energy-momentum tensor associated with this combination of matter is written as follows \begin{equation}T_{AB}=T_{AB}^{PF}+T_{AB}^{EM},\end{equation} where $T_{AB ^{PF}$ and $T_{AB ^{EM}$ are energy-momentum tensors of the perfect fluid and electromagnetic field, respectively.}}

elements.sent-2411-114: Using these components the field equations (\ref{eq_campo_tangente ) are written as \begin{equation}4\omega^2 f_R - f = -\pr{\frac{1}{2} (B^2 - E^2) - p} (f_L + 2f_T) + 2\pc{\rho + \frac{E_0^{2}}{2}} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2}(f_L + 2f_T)} - 2E^2 f_T, 2(2\omega^2 -m^2)f_R + f = \pr{\frac{1}{2}(B^2 - E^2) - p}(f_L + 2f_T) + 2 \pc{\frac{E_0^{2}}{2} - p}\pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2}(f_L + 2f_T)} - 2B^2 f_T, \label{2plus} -f = - \pr{\frac{1}{2} (B^2 - E^2) - p}(f_L + 2f_T) + 2 \pc{\frac{E_0^{2}}{2} + p} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2}(f_L + 2f_T)} - 2E^2 f_T. \label{3plus}\end{equation} Adding the equations (\ref{2plus ) and (\ref{3plus ), we obtain \begin{equation}(m^2 - 2\omega^2 )f_R = - E_0^{2} \pr{\kappa + \frac{1}{2}(f_L + 2f_T)} + f_T(E^2 + B^2).\end{equation}.}}}

elements.sent-2411-129: Therefore, assuming the scalar field as the content of matter, $f(R, \lm, T)$ gravity allows for a causal G\"{o del-type solution.}
elements.sent-2411-130: In other words, for this content of matter, no causality violation is permitted.
elements.sent-2411-131: It is important to note that if a combination of a perfect fluid plus a scalar field is taken as the matter content, similar results will be obtained.
elements.sent-2411-132: Furthermore, the results presented in this manuscript are a generalization of the study developed in references \cite{fr_and_godel, frt_and_godel for $f(R)$ gravity and $f(R, T)$ gravity, respectively.}

elements.sent-2411-137: Here a particular case of $f(R, \lm)$ gravity is considered.
elements.sent-2411-138: In this case the action of the gravitational field exhibits an exponential dependence on the standard Hilbert-Einstein Lagrangian density \cite{TH .}
elements.sent-2411-139: The action for this modified theory of gravity takes the following form \begin{equation}S= \int f(R,\lm) \sqrt{-g} \ d^4x , \label{58}\end{equation} where the function $f(R,\lm)$ is given as \bea f(R,\lm)=\Lambda \exp\left(\frac{1 {2\Lambda R+\frac{1 {\Lambda}\lm\right), \eea with $\Lambda$ being an arbitrary positive constant.}}}
elements.sent-2411-140: The standard general relativity action is recovered in the limit $(1/2\Lambda)R+(1/\Lambda)\lm \ll 0$.

elements.sent-2411-149: This result shows that exponential $f(R, \lm)$ gravity, with the electromagnetic field as the matter content, leads to the non-existence of G\"{o del's circles and, consequently, avoids the breakdown of G\"{o del-type causality.}}
elements.sent-2411-150: The present study focuses on $f(R, \lm, T)$ gravity.
elements.sent-2411-151: It is a gravitational theory that generalizes the coupling between geometry and matter.
elements.sent-2411-152: Analyzing the field equations, it is evident that the G\"{o del-type metric is a solution within this theory.}
elements.sent-2411-153: The investigation is carried out for different types of matter.
elements.sent-2411-154: Considering a perfect fluid as matter content, a non-causal solution is identified.
elements.sent-2411-155: This leads to the emergence of a critical radius that delineates a finite non-causal region.
elements.sent-2411-156: This critical radius depends on the parameters of the theory.
elements.sent-2411-157: In the search for a causal solution, an electromagnetic field is incorporated.
elements.sent-2411-158: As a consequence, a causal solution arises, which means that the critical radius tends to the infinity.
elements.sent-2411-159: Similar behavior occurs for a combination of a perfect fluid plus an electromagnetic field as matter content.
elements.sent-2411-160: A causal solution is also found in the case where the matter content is a scalar field.
elements.sent-2411-161: Furthermore, it is important to note that the theories $f(R)$, $f(R, T)$ and $f(R, L_m)$ and their causal or non-causal behavior are recovered in an appropriate limit.
elements.sent-2411-162: In addition, the results obtained here show that to preserve causality in $f(R, L_m, T)$ gravity a specific condition under the parameter $f_L$ is necessary.
elements.sent-2411-163: Additionally, the results for the modified theory of gravity can be directly compared to those of general relativity, as the G\"{o del-type solution is also an exact solution within general relativity.}
elements.sent-2411-164: While both theories permit causal and non-causal regions, the conditions leading to these regions differ.
elements.sent-2411-165: In $f(R, L_m, T)$ gravity, certain restrictions arise that depend on the parameters of the theory.
elements.sent-2411-166: The main finding - that $f(R, L_m, T)$ gravity allows the same exact solution as general relativity - serves as an important test for this theory, which was constructed to explain gravitational phenomena.
elements.sent-2411-167: Another important aspect to highlight concerns the critical radius.
elements.sent-2411-168: Our results indicate that, when considering different types of matter, the critical radius is finite in the case of a perfect fluid, while it becomes infinite for other forms of matter.
elements.sent-2411-169: Consequently, causal and non-causal, or physical and non-physical, regions are identified, which depend on the choice of matter.
elements.sent-2411-170: Although the critical radius is not a physical quantity in itself, it serves to delineate the physical and non-physical regions within this cosmological framework.
elements.sent-2411-171: Thus, the critical radius demonstrates behavior similar to that observed in general relativity.
elements.sent-2411-172: Another important point to note is that all results are derived for a general form of the function $f(R, L_m, T)$, making our findings applicable to all examples of this function.
elements.sent-2411-173: A similar study has been conducted for a specific case of $f(R, \lm)$ gravity, known as exponential $f(R, \lm)$ gravity.
elements.sent-2411-174: The analysis considered three different types of matter content: perfect fluid, scalar field, and electromagnetic field.
elements.sent-2411-175: In the first two cases, the same results as general relativity are obtained.
elements.sent-2411-176: However, for the electromagnetic field, an interesting condition emerges: to prevent the violation of causality, the cosmological constant must satisfy a specific limit.
elements.sent-2411-178: Gon\c{c alves thanks CAPES for financial support.}
